root_text.an10.5:1.1: Tatra kho āyasmā ānando bhikkhū āmantesi …pe…
root_text.an10.5:1.3: avippaṭisāre asati avippaṭisāravipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti pāmojjaṁ;
root_text.an10.5:1.4: pāmojje asati pāmojjavipannassa hatūpanisā hoti pīti;
root_text.an10.5:1.5: pītiyā asati pītivipannassa hatūpanisā hoti passaddhi;
root_text.an10.5:1.6: passaddhiyā asati passaddhivipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti sukhaṁ;
root_text.an10.5:1.7: sukhe asati sukhavipannassa hatūpaniso hoti sammāsamādhi;
root_text.an10.5:1.8: sammāsamādhimhi asati sammāsamādhivipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti yathābhūtañāṇadassanaṁ;
root_text.an10.5:1.9: yathābhūtañāṇadassane asati yathābhūtañāṇadassanavipannassa hatūpaniso hoti nibbidāvirāgo;
root_text.an10.5:1.10: nibbidāvirāge asati nibbidāvirāgavipannassa hatūpanisaṁ hoti vimuttiñāṇadassanaṁ.
root_text.an10.5:1.11: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, rukkho sākhāpalāsavipanno. Tassa papaṭikāpi na pāripūriṁ gacchati, tacopi … pheggupi … sāropi na pāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.an10.5:1.12: Evamevaṁ kho, āvuso, dussīlassa sīlavipannassa hatūpaniso hoti avippaṭisāro;
root_text.an10.5:2.1: Sīlavato, āvuso, sīlasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti avippaṭisāro;
root_text.an10.5:2.2: avippaṭisāre sati avippaṭisārasampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti pāmojjaṁ;
root_text.an10.5:2.3: pāmojje sati pāmojjasampannassa upanisasampannā hoti pīti;
root_text.an10.5:2.4: pītiyā sati pītisampannassa upanisasampannā hoti passaddhi;
root_text.an10.5:2.5: passaddhiyā sati passaddhisampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti sukhaṁ;
root_text.an10.5:2.6: sukhe sati sukhasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti sammāsamādhi;
root_text.an10.5:2.7: sammāsamādhimhi sati sammāsamādhisampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti yathābhūtañāṇadassanaṁ;
root_text.an10.5:2.8: yathābhūtañāṇadassane sati yathābhūtañāṇadassanasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti nibbidāvirāgo;
root_text.an10.5:2.9: nibbidāvirāge sati nibbidāvirāgasampannassa upanisasampannaṁ hoti vimuttiñāṇadassanaṁ.
root_text.an10.5:2.10: Seyyathāpi, āvuso, rukkho sākhāpalāsasampanno. Tassa papaṭikāpi pāripūriṁ gacchati, tacopi … pheggupi … sāropi pāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.an10.5:2.11: Evamevaṁ kho, āvuso, sīlavato sīlasampannassa upanisasampanno hoti avippaṭisāro;

translation_text.an10.5:1.1: There Venerable Ānanda addressed the mendicants …
translation_text.an10.5:1.2: “Reverends, an unethical person, who lacks ethics, has destroyed a vital condition for having no regrets.
translation_text.an10.5:1.3: When there are regrets, one who has regrets has destroyed a vital condition for joy.
translation_text.an10.5:1.4: When there is no joy, one who lacks joy has destroyed a vital condition for rapture.
translation_text.an10.5:1.5: When there is no rapture, one who lacks rapture has destroyed a vital condition for tranquility.
translation_text.an10.5:1.6: When there is no tranquility, one who lacks tranquility has destroyed a vital condition for bliss.
translation_text.an10.5:1.7: When there is no bliss, one who lacks bliss has destroyed a vital condition for right immersion.
translation_text.an10.5:1.8: When there is no right immersion, one who lacks right immersion has destroyed a vital condition for true knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an10.5:1.9: When there is no true knowledge and vision, one who lacks true knowledge and vision has destroyed a vital condition for disillusionment and dispassion.
translation_text.an10.5:1.10: When there is no disillusionment and dispassion, one who lacks disillusionment and dispassion has destroyed a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an10.5:1.11: Suppose there was a tree that lacked branches and foliage. Its shoots, bark, softwood, and heartwood would not grow to fullness.
translation_text.an10.5:1.12: In the same way, an unethical person, who lacks ethics, has destroyed a vital condition for having no regrets.
translation_text.an10.5:1.13: When there are regrets, one who has regrets has destroyed a vital condition for joy. …
translation_text.an10.5:1.14: One who lacks disillusionment and dispassion has destroyed a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an10.5:2.1: An ethical person, who has fulfilled ethics, has fulfilled a vital condition for not having regrets.
translation_text.an10.5:2.2: When there are no regrets, one who has no regrets has fulfilled a vital condition for joy.
translation_text.an10.5:2.3: When there is joy, one who has fulfilled joy has fulfilled a vital condition for rapture.
translation_text.an10.5:2.4: When there is rapture, one who has fulfilled rapture has fulfilled a vital condition for tranquility.
translation_text.an10.5:2.5: When there is tranquility, one who has fulfilled tranquility has fulfilled a vital condition for bliss.
translation_text.an10.5:2.6: When there is bliss, one who has fulfilled bliss has fulfilled a vital condition for right immersion.
translation_text.an10.5:2.7: When there is right immersion, one who has fulfilled right immersion has fulfilled a vital condition for true knowledge and vision.
translation_text.an10.5:2.8: When there is true knowledge and vision, one who has fulfilled true knowledge and vision has fulfilled a vital condition for disillusionment and dispassion.
translation_text.an10.5:2.9: When there is disillusionment and dispassion, one who has fulfilled disillusionment and dispassion has fulfilled a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an10.5:2.10: Suppose there was a tree that was complete with branches and foliage. Its shoots, bark, softwood, and heartwood would grow to fullness.
translation_text.an10.5:2.11: In the same way, an ethical person, who has fulfilled ethics, has fulfilled a vital condition for not having regrets.
translation_text.an10.5:2.12: When there are no regrets, one who has no regrets has fulfilled a vital condition for joy. …
translation_text.an10.5:2.13: One who has fulfilled disillusionment and dispassion has fulfilled a vital condition for knowledge and vision of freedom.”

reference_text.an10.5:1.1: bj23.12, dr24.7, ms17A10_23, msdiv5, pts-vp-pli5.6, sya24.7
